behaviors.description: Renamed files appear in their own category and are not also counted as a deletion plus an addition.
behaviors.justification: Real failures here commonly occur at the git subprocess boundary: rename detection and status formatting come from real git behavior, not just local categorization logic. A mocked parser can miss failures where real git reports rename/add-delete differently under actual repo history. Rejected higher levels: behavior text is purely git/CLI categorization with no AI/ML decision and no multi-agent sequence.
behaviors.test_description: Create a real temporary git repo, commit a file, rename it with content similarity preserved, run the CLI against the repo, and assert the report increments only the rename category (not added+deleted).

behaviors.description: Diffs that include binary files do not cause the tool to crash; binary files are counted in the output.
behaviors.justification: Crash risk is tied to real git diff/subprocess output characteristics for binary content (encoding, markers, exit behavior). In-process fakes often sanitize this and can hide true failures. Rejected higher levels: no LLM output dependency and no chained agentic steps in the behavior.
behaviors.test_description: In a real git repo, add/modify a binary file, run the CLI, and assert successful exit plus binary file inclusion in counts/categories.

behaviors.description: Running the tool on a repository that has no commits yet produces an empty report rather than an error.
behaviors.justification: This depends on real git behavior for an unborn HEAD and actual subprocess exit/status handling. Mocking repository responses can hide real command failures and stderr/exit-code edge cases. Rejected higher levels: behavior does not involve AI/ML output or multi-step agent pipeline.
behaviors.test_description: Initialize a real git repo with no commits, run the CLI, and assert it exits successfully and prints a valid empty structured report.

behaviors.description: The counts in the report match the actual change history of the repository being analyzed.
behaviors.justification: The phrase "actual change history of the repository" sets the failure boundary at real repository state plus git command interaction. Fixture-only tests can validate arithmetic but may miss mismatches caused by real history traversal, rename handling, or git invocation semantics. Rejected higher levels: no model judgment or agent chain is part of this behavior.
behaviors.test_description: Build a real repo history with known added/deleted/renamed/modified events across commits, run the CLI, and assert reported counts exactly match the constructed ground truth.

behaviors.description: The output is identical whether the changes being analyzed are staged or unstaged.
behaviors.justification: This behavior relies on real git index vs working-tree semantics and how the CLI reads them via subprocess calls. Mocked status outputs can hide real divergences in staged/unstaged interpretation. Rejected higher levels: behavior text contains no AI/ML dependency and no multi-step agentic orchestration.
behaviors.test_description: In a real repo, create equivalent staged and unstaged change scenarios, run the CLI in both modes, and assert byte-for-byte identical structured output.
